title: Pasania hystrix
taxonomy: Pasania hystrix is classified as a species of tree in the family Lauraceae. This genus is part of the larger family Lauraceae, which includes various species of trees commonly known as laurels. The Lauraceae family is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Pasania hystrix is native to Asia, specifically found in countries such as China and India. The classification of Pasania hystrix is further detailed in the botanical nomenclature system, where it is assigned the species epithet hystrix.

intro: Pasania hystrix, also known as the spiny pasania, is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae, native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties.
